about.closing: It is not designed as a feature checklist. It is designed to stay useful, reliable, and unobtrusive over long-term use.
about.description: The same conversation flows between your devices and chat apps — start at home, continue on the metro, finish on the couch — and it gets better the more you use it: memory, skills, and habits all accumulate together.
about.featureDailyDesc: A native desktop experience with system notifications, file paths, and local tools all working together; in service mode, any browser becomes the full UI.
about.featureMemoryDesc: Cross-session memory accumulates, gets organized while you're idle, and the things you've done turn into skills you can reuse next time.
about.featureReachDesc: One session across desktop, browser, and IM — start at home, continue on the metro, finish on the couch — no need to re-explain.
about.tagline: A desktop AI assistant that hands off across your devices and gets to know you better the more you use it.
about.title: About

approvalPanel.judgeExtraPromptPlaceholder: Optional: extra instructions for the judge (path whitelists, trusted commands…)
approvalPanel.protectedPathsDesc: Tool calls hitting these paths force approval and cannot be AllowAlways'd. Supports ~/ expansion, directory prefix (trailing /), and *.pattern globs.
approvalPanel.smartDesc: In Smart session mode, the model's self-confidence tag or an independent judge model decides approvals.
approvalPanel.timeoutDesc: When the user doesn't respond within this many seconds, fall back to the action below. 0 = wait forever.
approvalPanel.timeoutEnabledDesc: Off by default. When off, approval dialogs wait until you respond.
approvalPanel.title: Permission
approvalPanel.yoloCliActive: ⚠️ The CLI flag --dangerously-skip-all-approvals is active and cannot be disabled from the GUI.
approvalPanel.yoloDesc: When on, all tools run without approval; only Plan Mode can still block. Protected-path / dangerous-command matches log a warning but execute.

settings.approvalTimeoutActionDesc: What to do when approval wait times out
settings.approvalTimeoutDesc: Max time to wait for a tool approval response. Set to 0 to disable
settings.asyncToolsAutoBackgroundDesc: Sync calls exceeding this duration are automatically transferred to a background job. 0 = disable auto-transfer.
settings.asyncToolsDesc: Long-running tools (exec, web_search, image_generate) can be transferred to background jobs so the conversation can continue. Results are auto-injected when ready.
settings.asyncToolsEnabledDesc: Master switch. When off, run_in_background is ignored and every tool runs synchronously.
settings.asyncToolsInlineBytesDesc: Result bytes inlined in the completion notification. Larger results are spooled with a head/tail preview.
settings.asyncToolsJobStatusMaxWaitDesc: Upper bound for a single job_status(block=true) call. Only applied when max job duration = 0; otherwise max job duration takes precedence.
settings.asyncToolsMaxJobDesc: Hard limit on how long a background job may run before being killed. 0 = no async-job limit; individual tools may still enforce their own timeouts.
settings.asyncToolsOrphanGraceDesc: Spool files not referenced by any DB row are cleaned up after this many hours. Grace window prevents races with in-flight writes.
settings.asyncToolsRetentionDesc: Completed job rows and their spool files are purged after this many days. 0 = keep forever.

awareness.desc: Give this chat a dynamic view of what the user is doing in other parallel sessions.
awareness.llmWarning: LLM Digest mode runs an extra side_query per user turn (throttled to min_interval_secs). Expect extra API cost.
awareness.perSessionHint: Per-session overrides are also available via the /awareness command or API.
awareness.title: Behavior Awareness

browser.connectHelp: Already running Chrome with --remote-debugging-port=9222? Attach instead of launching a new instance.
browser.deleteConfirmDesc: All cookies, logins and cached data for this profile will be removed. This cannot be undone.
browser.deleteDisabledActive: Stop the browser before deleting its profile
browser.desc: Hope Agent can launch and drive a dedicated Chrome instance for you. Create separate profiles to keep logins (Google, GitHub, work tools…) isolated — the agent and you share the same session, so whatever you sign into, the agent can operate with.
browser.doctorChromeMissing: No Chrome detected on the standard debug port
browser.doctorChromeMissingHint: Launch a separate Chrome (its own user-data-dir; your daily browsing data is not touched).
browser.doctorNoBinaryHint: Install Google Chrome system-wide, or click to download a portable Chromium runtime (~150 MB) under ~/.hope-agent/browser/runtime/.
browser.executableHint: Leave empty to auto-detect. Only set if you want a specific Chrome/Chromium build.
browser.headlessHint: Hide the browser window. Off by default so you can sign in.
browser.launchHelp: Pick a profile below, then click Launch. A Chrome window will open — log in once and your cookies are kept in the profile directory for next time.
browser.modeStandaloneHint: hope-agent maintains an isolated Chrome instance. Recommended for automation.
browser.modeUserChromeHint: Spawn or attach to a Chrome you use every day. Uses a separate user-data-dir so your real browsing data is not touched.
browser.profileHint: Profile '{{name}}' — cookies and logins will persist.
browser.profileNoneHint: Without a profile, Chrome runs with a throwaway session. Create a profile to keep logins.
browser.profilesEmpty: No profiles yet. Create one above to save logins between sessions.
browser.profilesHelp: Each profile is a separate Chrome user-data-dir. Create one per workflow (e.g. personal-google, work-github) and sign in ahead of time — the agent uses whichever profile you launched with.

spawnUserChrome.confirmBodyIdle: hope-agent will start a new Chrome with its own user-data-dir under ~/.hope-agent/browser/user-attach/. Continue?
spawnUserChrome.confirmBodyRunning: Chrome appears to be running. hope-agent will start a separate Chrome instance with its own user-data-dir, so your daily browsing is unaffected. Continue?

settings.canvasAutoShowDesc: Automatically open the preview panel when canvas is created or updated
settings.canvasClearAllConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all canvas projects? This cannot be undone.
settings.canvasDesc: Interactive canvas tool: AI can create and manage HTML/Markdown/charts/slides and other visual content, rendered live in a preview panel.
settings.channelsDesc: Manage Telegram, WeChat, Feishu, Discord, Slack and other IM channel integrations
settings.charLimitExceeded: Exceeded limit, excess content will be truncated
settings.chatAutoExpandThinkingDesc: Automatically expand thinking blocks in chat bubbles. When off, click to expand manually.
settings.chatAutoSendDesc: Automatically send queued messages after reply finishes. When off, message is filled back to input.
settings.chatDisplayModeDesc: Choose the default layout for chat messages.
settings.configBackupsDesc: Every settings change is snapshotted automatically. Roll back to any previous version (last 50 kept).
settings.configBackupsRestoreDesc: Will restore config to {{timestamp}} ({{kind}} / {{category}}). The current state is snapshotted first, so you can undo the rollback.
settings.contextCompactCacheTtlDesc: Skip pruning/summarization if last compaction was within this time. Preserves API prompt cache. 0 = disabled
settings.contextCompactDesc: Automatically compress conversation context to prevent exceeding model limits
settings.contextCompactEnabledDesc: Automatically trim old tool results and summarize old messages when conversation gets long
settings.contextCompactHardClearRatioDesc: Replace old tool results with placeholders when still exceeding this ratio after soft trim
settings.contextCompactIdentifierInstructionsPlac: e.g.: Preserve all file paths and URLs exactly, other IDs may be abbreviated
settings.contextCompactKeepAssistantsDesc: Tool results from the last N assistant replies are never pruned
settings.contextCompactMaxSummaryCharsDesc: Maximum characters for compaction summary (4000–64000). Increase for richer context retention, but the summary itself takes more space
settings.contextCompactMaxToolResultShareDesc: Max share of context window a single tool result can occupy (10%–60%). Increase to preserve larger file reads and web fetches
settings.contextCompactMicrocompactEnabledDesc: Zero-cost clearing of old ephemeral tool results (ls/grep/find etc.), runs before other compression tiers
settings.contextCompactMicrocompactToolsDesc: Old results from selected tools will be automatically cleared
settings.contextCompactMinPrunableCharsDesc: Skip hard clear if total prunable chars below this value
settings.contextCompactPreserveTurnsDesc: Keep the last N conversation turns verbatim during summarization
settings.contextCompactReactiveMicrocompactDesc: Between tool loop rounds, when usage crosses the threshold, clear ephemeral tool_results (ls/grep/find/…). Tier 0 — zero cost, keeps prompt cache valid.
settings.contextCompactReactiveTriggerRatioDesc: Usage ratio that triggers reactive microcompaction (default 75%).
settings.contextCompactSoftTrimMaxChars: Min result size for soft trim
settings.contextCompactSoftTrimMaxCharsDesc: Tool results smaller than this are skipped during soft trim
settings.contextCompactSoftTrimRatioDesc: Start head+tail trimming of old tool results when context usage exceeds this ratio
settings.contextCompactSummarizationModelCacheHin: Uses prompt cache sharing with conversation model, ~90% cache hit rate for minimal token cost
settings.contextCompactSummarizationModelCustomHi: Uses dedicated model for summarization. On failure, automatically falls back to conversation model with cache sharing
settings.contextCompactSummarizationThresholdDesc: Trigger LLM summarization of old messages when still exceeding this ratio after pruning
settings.contextCompactToolPolicyDesc: Control how each tool's results are handled during context compression
settings.contextCompactToolsDenyDesc: Results from these tools are always kept in full, comma separated
settings.coreMemoryAgentDesc: Rules and preferences specific to this agent, always visible in conversations
settings.coreMemoryGlobalDesc: Rules and preferences shared across all agents, always visible in conversations
settings.coreMemoryPlaceholder:
Write rules and preferences you want the AI to always remember...
e.g.: Reply in English, no code comments, use pnpm only
settings.dangerousCliLocked: Active via CLI flag --dangerously-skip-all-approvals; this toggle is read-only for the current launch. Restart without the flag to disable.
settings.dangerousConfirmAck: I understand the risks and consent to skipping every tool approval
settings.dangerousConfirmBody: The following actions will run without any approval prompt:
settings.dangerousConfirmNote: Plan Mode's tool-type restrictions still apply. You can turn this switch off here at any time.
settings.dangerousIntro: One-click switch to bypass every tool approval. Extreme risk — enable only on fully trusted local machines.
settings.dangerousSwitchDesc: When on, every tool call runs without prompting. Overrides all global, session, and channel auto-approve settings.
settings.defaultAgentDesc: Used when no agent is specified, falling back through Project → IM channel → this default.
settings.deferredToolsEnabledDesc: Enable tool_search-based loading. Tools remain injected until you defer them below. Takes effect on the next conversation.
settings.deferredToolsPerToolDesc: Turn on only the tools whose schemas should be discovered via tool_search instead of sent up front.
settings.devClearAllDesc: Clear all data and reset to defaults
settings.devClearCronDesc: Delete all scheduled tasks and run logs
settings.devClearSessionsDesc: Delete all chat history and attachments
settings.devConfirmDesc: This action cannot be undone. The app will restart to reinitialize. Are you sure?
settings.devResetConfigDesc: Reset all system settings to defaults
settings.devWarningDesc: The following actions will permanently delete data and cannot be undone. The app will restart automatically after execution.

dreaming.candidateLimitDesc: Max candidates sent to the LLM per cycle.
dreaming.cronExprInvalid: Cron expression is invalid. Saving anyway — the scheduler will skip until you fix it.
dreaming.cronTriggerEnabledDesc: Fire on a cron expression in addition to (or instead of) the idle trigger.
dreaming.desc: Periodically scan recent memories and pin the most evergreen ones into core memory. Runs offline; never blocks chat.
dreaming.idleMinutesDesc: How long to wait without user activity before firing.
dreaming.idleTriggerEnabledDesc: Automatically start a cycle after the app sits idle for the configured number of minutes.
dreaming.manualEnabledDesc: Lets you trigger a cycle from Dashboard → Dreaming. Disabling hides the button without disabling the feature.
dreaming.maxPromoteDesc: Hard cap on how many memories one cycle can promote.
dreaming.minScoreDesc: Only memories scored at or above this threshold (0–1) are pinned.
dreaming.narrativeMaxTokensDesc: Output budget for the narrative side query.
dreaming.narrativeModelDesc: Override the model used for narrative scoring. Empty falls back to the active chat / recap model chain.
dreaming.narrativeTimeoutSecsDesc: Side query is aborted after this many seconds.
dreaming.noCycleYet: No cycles run yet in this process. The diary archive on disk is unaffected.
dreaming.scopeDaysDesc: How many days of recent memories to scan.
dreaming.title: Dreaming

settings.imageGenPriorityHint: Providers are tried in priority order with automatic failover

memoryBudget.agentOverrideDesc: Inherits the global Memory Budget by default. Toggle off to use an agent-specific budget.
memoryBudget.coreMemoryFileCharsDesc: Independent cap for each memory.md file (Global / Agent). Content beyond the cap is truncated in the prompt but preserved on disk. Default 8000.
memoryBudget.desc: Caps the total character count of the Memory section (Global memory.md / Agent memory.md / SQLite memories / Guidelines) and the per-layer limits inside it. Lowering the total frees budget for the live conversation context.
memoryBudget.sqliteEntryMaxCharsDesc: Upper bound on the first line of each rendered SQLite memory bullet, preventing one long entry from crowding out same-section peers. Default 500.
memoryBudget.title: Memory Budget
memoryBudget.totalCharsDesc: Hard upper bound for the entire Memory section. Guidelines are reserved first; remaining layers drop in priority order. Default 10000.

settings.memoryDedupAdvancedDesc: Adjust RRF similarity thresholds for memory deduplication. Higher values make it easier to detect duplicates.
settings.memoryDesc: Persistent memories that help the AI remember context across conversations
settings.memoryDuplicateFound: Similar memories found — do you want to add anyway or update an existing one?
settings.memoryEmbeddingAutoDesc: Automatically selects the best available embedding provider (local model first, then reuses configured LLM API keys). No extra setup needed.
settings.memoryEmbeddingCacheDesc: Caches computed vectors to avoid redundant API calls for repeated text, saving cost.
settings.memoryEmbeddingDesc: Configure embedding models for semantic memory search. Without this, only keyword search is available.
settings.memoryEmbeddingEnabledDesc: Use embedding models to find semantically similar memories
settings.memoryEmbeddingLegacyProvider: This is still using the old {{provider}} configuration. Choose an explicit provider and save it to avoid vector-space changes from automatic switching.

settings.memorySearchTuningDesc: Adjust hybrid search weights, temporal decay, and diversity reranking parameters.

settings.recapFacetConcurrencyDesc: Parallel side-query calls when extracting per-session facets. Lower if you hit rate limits. Default 4.
settings.recapMaxSessionsDesc: Hard cap on how many sessions a single report analyzes. Default 500.

settings.sandboxReadOnlyDesc: Mount root filesystem as read-only, only /workspace and /tmp are writable

settings.serverEmbeddedBindDesc: Default 127.0.0.1 for localhost only. Set to 0.0.0.0 to expose to the network

skillsAutoReview.description: Skip the draft step — newly distilled skills go live immediately.
skillsAutoReview.hint: Off (default): new skills land as drafts and need your approval first.
skillsAutoReview.label: Auto-activate new skills

settings.skillsDesc: Skills provide domain expertise for the AI assistant and are automatically injected into the system prompt. Place skill folders in:
settings.skillsDraftsDesc: Your Agent identified these reusable patterns after recent conversations and created draft skills. Activate to make them available in the system prompt.

recentRejects.title: Recent reject reasons
recentRejects.empty: No rejections recorded yet. Whenever any gate stops a review from producing a draft, the reason lands here.

curator.title: Draft consolidation
curator.help: Find clusters of draft skills that overlap topically and merge them in one click — keep one, discard the rest. Read-only scan, no LLM call.
curator.idle: Not scanned yet. Click "Scan" to look for overlapping drafts.
curator.noClusters: Scanned {{count}} drafts. No obvious overlap found.
curator.autoNoticeBody: {{proposals}} cluster(s) across {{scanned}} drafts are ready to review below.

resetAll.label: Reset everything
resetAll.confirm: Reset every custom field to defaults?

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.subtitle: Decides which conversations even reach the review pipeline. Pure-chat turns are skipped by default.

quality.title: Quality floor
quality.subtitle: After the model's call, these floors decide whether the suggestion actually lands as a draft.

advanced.title: Advanced (prompt overrides)
advanced.subtitle: Override the built-in review prompt, append custom reject categories, pin a dedicated review model. Backend gates 4 / 5 still apply regardless.
advanced.confirm: Advanced overrides can lower the model-side quality bar. Confirm you understand the trade-off.

rejectReasons.post_lint_session_recap: Body reads like a conversation recap
rejectReasons.post_lint_too_abstract: Body lacks concrete commands / paths / code
rejectReasons.gate4_not_class_level: Model flagged itself as not class-level
rejectReasons.patch_target_not_found: Patch target not found in existing skill

requireToolUse.label: Require tool use this turn
requireToolUse.help: When on, pure-chat turns (zero tool use) never trigger review. Recommended.

correctionSignalEnabled.label: Treat user corrections as a trigger
correctionSignalEnabled.help: Two user messages within 30 seconds (likely a correction) also fire one review.

toolUseThreshold.label: Tool-use count threshold
toolUseThreshold.help: Triggers when the round's tool calls reach this count. Set 0 to disable.

cooldownSecs.label: Per-session cooldown
cooldownSecs.help: Minimum interval between automatic reviews for the same session.

tokenThreshold.label: Token threshold
tokenThreshold.help: Round-level input+output tokens. Also requires at least one tool call when the require-tool-use switch is on.

messageThreshold.label: Message threshold
messageThreshold.help: Number of new messages this round. Same tool-use requirement applies.

minReuseProbability.label: Minimum reuse probability
minReuseProbability.help: Model's self-estimated probability the skill gets used in the next 30 days. Drafts below this floor are discarded.

topKForDedup.label: Dedup candidates (Top-K)
topKForDedup.help: Feed the top-K closest existing skills' full bodies to the model so it can prefer patching over creating.

discardBlacklistDays.label: Discard blacklist window (days)
discardBlacklistDays.help: Topics you discarded within this window will not be auto-created again. 0 disables.

minMessageCount.label: Minimum message count
minMessageCount.help: Skip review when the recent transcript has fewer than this many messages.

sessionRecapThreshold.label: Session-recap word threshold
sessionRecapThreshold.help: Reject when this many session-recap markers ("today", "this conversation", "上面", etc.) appear in the body.

minSteps.label: Minimum steps in body
minSteps.help: Total list-item count in the body must be at least this many.

maxSteps.label: Maximum steps in body
maxSteps.help: Bodies above this length are usually conversation transcripts dressed up as a procedure.

reviewModel.label: Dedicated review model
reviewModel.help: Format: provider:model. Empty reuses the main chat's cached prefix.

extraRejectCategories.label: Extra reject categories
extraRejectCategories.help: Append your own reject categories on top of the built-in six (e.g. "MEDICAL-ADVICE", "LEGAL-ADVICE"). Press Enter to add.

reviewSystemOverride.label: Override review system prompt
reviewSystemOverride.help: Replaces the built-in review system prompt verbatim; leave empty to use the built-in. Backend hard floors still apply.

autoCuratorEnabled.label: Run curator on a schedule
autoCuratorEnabled.help: When on, Hope Agent periodically scans draft skills for overlap and surfaces merge suggestions here.

autoCuratorIntervalDays.label: Curator interval
autoCuratorIntervalDays.help: Days between automatic draft-consolidation scans. The backend re-reads this setting each cycle.

skillsEvolutionHero.body: Hope Agent doesn't stay still. Every so often it replays your chats, turns the things you keep doing into reusable skills — so the next time it comes up, the agent already knows how.
skillsEvolutionHero.note: On by default · grows more capable the more you use it. Turn off to keep things as-is — no new skills will form on their own.
skillsEvolutionHero.title: Distill what you keep doing into reusable skills

hooks.title: Hooks
hooks.intro: Run custom commands, HTTP calls, prompts or sub-agents at lifecycle events. Field-level compatible with Claude Code hooks.

hooks.allowProjectScopeDesc: Lets a working directory's .hope-agent/hooks.json and hooks.local.json add hooks — handy for sharing with your team. Off by default because these can run commands on their own, so switch it on only for projects you trust.
hooks.scopeNote: This panel edits user-scope hooks. Project (.hope-agent/hooks.json, repo-shared), local (hooks.local.json) and managed (/etc/hope-agent/hooks.json) scopes are file-based and merged on top.
hooks.empty: No hooks configured. Pick an event above to add one.
hooks.matcherPlaceholder: tool name / source (regex ok), empty = all
hooks.slowHandlerWarning: This handler runs an LLM / sub-agent inline and will delay every time this blocking event fires. Prefer command / http here, or move it to an observation event (e.g. PostToolUse).

manual.dockerContext: Hope Agent is running in a Docker container. Self-update can't replace the image — upgrade by pulling a new tag and recreating the container.
manual.dockerText:
Hope Agent is running in a Docker container ({{from}} → {{to}}).

Upgrade by pulling the new image and recreating the container:

    docker pull ghcr.io/shiwenwen/hope-agent:v{{to}}
    docker compose up -d

or pin to `latest` if your compose file uses it.
manual.context: Hope Agent cannot pick an automated upgrade path for this install. Decide how to proceed.

manual.openReleasesManual: Open release page in browser (manual download)
manual.forceSelfContained: Try the self-contained bare-binary swap anyway
